feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: While posting from BAPI getting error of valuation type
entry.content:
Dear support team ,

i have a scenario where the same material against the same purchase orders i"m able to post from MIGO but not able to post from BAPI .

getting below error and also save BAPI in test directory for the reference. TEST CASE SAVE IN QUALITY SERVER

  BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE 

ERROR:   E M7 360 Material document data and PO data do not match (Valuation Type)

entry.title: when posting using 543 movement then sys is considering the standard price not moving average price
entry.content: Created a refurbished with material"s valuation Type R( Valuation type R has price indicator V) and component"s valuation type B/D (Valuation type B/D has price indicator S). But when receiving the finished production using 543 movement then system is considering the standard price not moving average price.

entry.title: Major bug in SAP. We do not need SP Key rather we should have source plant and destination field
entry.content: Currently SP Key field in MRP 2 in MM03 is created unnecessarily since we do not have any field called source plant and destination plant in S4 Hana. There are lot of problems for creation of SP key which is unique to company based on source plant and destination plant. For every new plant transfer, new SP key needs to be created and maintained. SAP has created this problem. Instead for every material, source plant and destination plant fields should have been given in MRP 2 view in MM03.

entry.title: SAFT-PT - Error - 'InvoiceCustomerIDConstraint'
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm validating the SAFT-PT file, but I received the following message:

"Error: KeyRef Constraint 'InvoiceCustomerIDConstraint' violated: 74XXXX/XX (customer number)."

Upon reviewing the file, I noticed that the Customer ID appears in the Master File with the same number but without the "/XX" suffix. Before taking any action, I checked previous SAFT-PT reports and confirmed that they include invoices with this Customer ID, even though it's still not present in the Master File.

Does anyone have any insight into this issue?

Thank you,
Bartomeu

entry.title: Migration to RISE with SAP with Selective Data Transition: Flexibility is the Key
entry.content:
In an era where digital transformation is not just a goal but a necessity, many companies face a central question: How can we successfully move to SAP S/4HANA and position the entire organization for the future? For many, the answer lies in RISE with SAP – SAP’s comprehensive cloud offering. It combines software, infrastructure, and services in one integrated package. But the journey is complex: processes, data, and systems must be securely migrated to the new environment without operational risks.

This article explains how the Selective Data Transition (SDT) approach supports this transition – enabling companies to migrate to the cloud quickly, efficiently, and flexibly.

RISE with SAP: More Than Just Cloud Infrastructure

RISE with SAP provides businesses with a clear outlook for the future: a flexible, cloud-based ERP environment with access to cutting-edge technologies like artificial intelligence, automation, real-time analytics, and process intelligence. But it’s about more than technological innovation – RISE represents a holistic approach to business transformation.

Key benefits for companies include:

  • Flexible operating models: SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA On-Premise in a modular concept
  • Standardization & harmonization: Consolidate and optimize processes and systems
  • Scalable innovation: Rapid access to new features and innovations via the cloud

The goal is to pave the way for an agile, scalable, and innovation-ready ERP environment. But technological capabilities alone aren’t enough to overcome the associated challenges. This is where Selective Data Transition (SDT) comes in: a selective migration approach that combines the strengths of the traditional Greenfield and Brownfield methods – and delivers results faster.

The Challenges of Traditional Migration Approaches

When migrating to SAP S/4HANA, the how is just as important as the what. One of the main challenges is to ensure a seamless transition without disrupting ongoing operations. This forces companies to ask: Should we completely redesign the ERP system from scratch (Greenfield), or will a technical system conversion (Brownfield) suffice?

A closer look at the traditional approaches can help guide the decision:

  • Greenfield approach: This method involves a complete redesign of the system landscape. Companies have the opportunity to redefine their processes – especially useful when outdated or inefficient structures exist. However, this approach requires significant investment and involves long implementation times.
  • Brownfield approach: This involves a technical conversion of the existing system. Most processes and data are retained, allowing for faster implementation. However, it carries the risk of carrying over legacy issues that can hinder innovation. However, it carries several risks: legacy issues from previous system versions may be transferred unchanged, limiting future innovation. Moreover, since there is typically no selective data cleansing or reduction, large systems tend to remain large – leading to increased complexity, higher operational costs, and limited agility in the long run.

Beyond these two traditional paths, Selective Data Transition (SDT) has emerged as a forward-looking solution – bridging the gap between Greenfield and Brownfield and enabling companies to shape their transition to SAP S/4HANA in an innovative and sustainable way.

Selective Data Transition: A Hybrid Migration Approach

Selective Data Transition (SDT) combines the best of both worlds. But what exactly makes this approach so unique?

  • Selective data transfer: Companies can decide exactly which data and processes to carry over to the new system. This allows for outdated or redundant information to be discarded, while retaining business-critical data. At the same time, this approach enables companies to systematically migrate the data that will be relevant for future machine learning and AI applications – a crucial step toward becoming a truly data-driven enterprise. In addition, the data can be freely adopted to enable further optimizations as described below.
  • Business process optimization: Unlike pure system conversion, SDT allows for a detailed analysis and targeted improvement of existing processes. Companies can eliminate inefficient workflows and implement best practices – actively shaping their digital transformation.
  • Harmonization and consolidation: Many organizations operate multiple SAP systems in parallel – often due to mergers or international expansion. SDT enables the consolidation of various systems into one unified S/4HANA environment, simplifying the IT landscape and reducing costs.
  • Tailored to business needs: Every company has specific requirements for its IT environment. SDT enables the creation of customized migration paths that align with individual business models and structures.

This hybrid approach provides the flexibility companies need to build a sustainable, future-proof SAP landscape.

RISE with SAP and SDT: A Powerful Combination

Cloud migration is a central component of many companies’ digital strategies. A strategic move via SDT offers several key advantages:

  • Flexibility and tailored strategy: While a full Greenfield approach often implies a radical change, SDT enables a gradual transition to the cloud. Companies can align their migration roadmap with their business strategy.
  • Cost efficiency through cloud technologies: Moving to RISE with SAP allows companies to benefit from a subscription-based model instead of large one-time investments. At the same time, selective migration reduces effort and associated costs.
  • Business continuity: One major challenge of migration projects is minimizing downtime. SDT enables a controlled transfer of data and processes, allowing business operations to continue largely uninterrupted during the transition.
  • Future-proof through innovation: RISE with SAP provides access to modern cloud technologies such as AI-powered analytics, automation, and IoT integration. With SDT, companies can strategically integrate these innovations into their existing IT landscape – without risking a complete system overhaul.

Ready for the Future

The question is no longer if you should migrate to SAP S/4HANA, but how. Companies looking to leverage the full potential of the cloud should define their migration strategy now. The SDT approach offers a flexible, future-ready way to modernize IT landscapes and strategically position businesses for what lies ahead.

Those who act early will be best positioned to fully capitalize on the competitive edge offered by digital transformation.

entry.title: Transaktion SMX - Fehlender Löschen Button
entry.content:
Guten Tag, 

nach dem Releaseupgrade S/4 Hana 2022 SPS5 findet wir den Button "löschen" nicht mehr.
Job auf Datenbank löschen.

Kann mir jemand Hinweise und Tipps angeben?

Besten Dank

entry.title: Sending PR from Ariba to S/4HANA
entry.content: We are implementing a scenario where the PR is created in Ariba and sent to S/4HANA for the rest of the flow to be done in S/4HANA however Ariba tenant has two ERP systems registered and we need to specify one of them. The creation of PR does not include a field to specify which ERP system. Are there any parameters that need to be configured in Ariba for this specific purpose?

entry.title: Getting ICMENOSESSION error while calling SAP S4 webservice from SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We are working on scenarios in SAP CPI to call SAP S4 webservice to get the data in request/reply way. There are multiple calls happening to S4 webservice using SOAP 1.x receiver adapter.

FLOW:  SAP CPI <-> SAP SCC <-> SAP S4 (on-prem)

Modernize RFC Receiver Communications into API-Based Protocols in Cloud Integration. Link to understand the scenario in details: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/modernize-rfc-receiver.html

In S4, webservice is linked to function module based on the request payload we are sending from CPI function module is getting executed based on the logic written in it then giving back the response immediately.

Problem statement:

  • Webservice calls are getting executed successfully for few initial calls. Some of the execution whole integration is getting executed successfully without failure.
    But sometimes suddenly getting failed with error: "org.apache.cxf.interceptor.Fault: Could not send Message., cause: org.apache.cxf.transport.http.HTTPException: HTTP response '400: Bad Request. <!-- This error page was generated by ICM (-)! -->
    <!-- Error code: ICMENOSESSION "
  • We are not able to see any logs in SRT_MONI or SXMB_MONI about this failure in S4. Also tried to check in SRT_UTIL but no luck.

From our end we have tried to execute the integration by doing multiple changes:

  • Made HTTP session reuse 'none' in CPI and enableSession parameter false in S4 wsdl setting. (KBA:

    https://me.sap.com/notes/0003419590)

  • Also tried to remove allow chunking in SAP CPI SOAP adapter configuration.
  • All cases we are throwing same error.

Please suggest further if you also encountered similar kind of issues. let me know possible resolution of this issue.

Regards,

Souvik

entry.title: We want to assign SAP_ALL but remove TR, movement
entry.content:
Hi Team,


Please suggest, We assign SAP_ALL to user but remove from his/her id TR movement.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Erro:No form was assigned to case type FDCA and procedure B2B-EXIST. About Credit DCD
entry.content:
Dear All,

My sales orders are showing up in VKM* transaction codes, but they are not visible in UKM_MY_DCDS. Therefore, I used the transaction code UKM_DCD_SETUP_CHECK to check the configuration. There were three error messages. Could you please advise me on how to configure it correctly?

entry.title: sap ehs incident management task program
entry.content:
Is it ok to run below two programs before  task configuration and fiori activation. it works good that way

 

R_EHFND_XPRA_WFF_MIGRATION
R_EHFND_WFF_RESTART_PROCESSES

entry.title: \/SCWM/TO_CREATE OVER PICKING
entry.content:
We developed a PDA program that picks using CALL FUNCTION /SCWM/TO_CREATE.
However, OVER PICKING sometimes occurs.
Can you make sure that it doesn"t happen?

  DATA lt_create_exc TYPE /scwm/tt_conf_exc.

  IF ls_item-fr_huident NE ls_item-to_huident
    AND ls_item-to_huident IS NOT INITIAL.

    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_create_exc ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_create_exc>).
    <fs_create_exc>-exccode   = 'CHHU'.
    <fs_create_exc>-buscon    = 'TPI'.
    <fs_create_exc>-exec_step = '02'.
  ENDIF.


  DATA: lt_ltap_vb  TYPE /scwm/tt_ltap_vb,
        lt_bapiret  TYPE bapirettab,
        lv_severity TYPE bapi_mtype.

  SET UPDATE TASK LOCAL.
  CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/TO_CREATE'
    EXPORTING
      iv_lgnum       = ls_item-lgnum
      iv_update_task = abap_off
      iv_commit_work = abap_off      
      iv_wtcode      = space  
      it_create      = lt_create
      it_create_exc  = lt_create_exc
    IMPORTING
      et_ltap_vb     = lt_ltap_vb
      et_bapiret     = lt_bapiret
      ev_severity    = lv_severity.

entry.title: ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS Custom Fields and Logic
entry.content:
Hi 
I have extended S/4 system by using the "Custom Fields and Logic" app - this works nicely. Without any code at all.

But when I then use the "Custom Code Migration Projects" to analyze the system these new object are in that list - how should I handle that - because I would like to have a clean-code system as possible ?

Please advise

Thanks

Lars

entry.title: 発注伝票納入先住所について
entry.content:
仕入先直送受注伝票ヘッダの「出荷先コード」を変更し、同時に変更後の出荷先の「住所」も変更された後、発注明細「50」のみの「納入先住所コード」が空白の状態となっていました。

※詳細は添付ファイル「【問い合わせ資料】発注伝票納入先住所について.xlsx」をご参照ください。

entry.title: 発注伝票納入先住所について Regarding the delivery address on the order slip
entry.content:
仕入先直送受注伝票ヘッダの「出荷先コード」を変更し、同時に変更後の出荷先の「住所」も変更された後、発注明細「50」のみの「納入先住所コード」が空白の状態となっていました。
※詳細は添付ファイル「【問い合わせ資料】発注伝票納入先住所について.xlsx」をご参照ください。
The "shipping destination code" in the header of the direct order invoice from the supplier was changed, and at the same time, the address of the changed shipping destination was also updated. However, the "delivery address code" in the order detail "50" remained blank. *For more details, please refer to the attached file "[Inquiry Material] About Delivery Address of Order Invoice.xlsx."
上記の状況はSAP標準機能の不具合のようので、根本原因についてご確認いただけますと幸いです。
The above situation seems to be a bug in the SAP standard functionality, so I would appreciate it if you could check on the root cause.

entry.title: OSS Note 2633052 for S4Core 108 available?
entry.content:
Hello,

there is the OSS note 2633052 which is for the software components S4Core 101 and S4Core 102.

Our system has the component S4Core 108, but we would like to implement the functionality from the above mentioned note.

The requirement is to be able to preview Sales Billing document form layouts during their creation in VF01 and VF04, before they are posted.

Is there a newer note for this functionality?

Thanks and kind regards

Andrej

entry.title: SAP Web dispatcher error
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using permission table (via parameter "wdisp/permission_table") in the new SAP Web Dispatcher we installed and we want the case sensitivity not to be active. How can we go about this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Final settlement is not considering the previous settlement amount in Multi Payee Condition contract
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have one issue in CCM- Condition contracts Management.

In Multi Payee Scenario, System is not adjusting the amount in the final settlement considering the partial settlement amount which was already settled but creating final settlement document with exact amount like in partial settlement document and not determining RES2 condition type.
Final settlement is considering previous settlement amount only when the Bill to party number is same as Sold to Party and determining RES2 in final settlement document.

Request you to share the inputs.

#Settlement Management   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales   SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Need explanation on the MENGE value in the OSS note 3382819
entry.content:
hi,

In the OSS note 3382819 - MR11/MR11SHOW - Calculation logic for GR/IR clearing, the calculation details for the MR11 cancellation amount are explained.

But in the formula, a variable MENGE is used but this one is not explained. Can someone help me to understand the calculation ?

Thanks in advance for your feedback,

Regards, 
Céline JOMARD
SAP FI specialist

entry.title: Issue in UNIT_CONVERSION in CDS View Entity
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to perform unit conversion in CDS view entities in version 75I. So, trying to use unit_conversion, however it ends with SQL code 719.
My CDS view:

Error with execution:

Database returned the SQL code 719. Error text: currency/unit conversion error: search table error: Conversion( "COL$11$" ),
row 0: could not determine valid conversion rate for value(s) 7 from unit 'ST' to unit 'KAR' for client '010'

Both these unit are part of T006 table.

Could you please provide valuable inputs to solve the same.

Regards,

JR
